mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in months with PAYEMS growth below 50k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 2.5% triggers central bank tightening expectations, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-inflation periods exceed 1.5% despite CPIAUCSL > 2.5% (z-score > 2.0).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening supply-side capacity and demand, creating a negative feedback loop that reduces S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in periods with INDPRO/HOUST declines exceed 1.0% despite the signal (z-score < -1.5).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising FEDFUNDS rates reduce corporate borrowing and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in FEDFUNDS > 2.0% periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in residential construction (RSAFS) indicates strong housing demand, which correlates with higher consumer confidence and corporate investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in RSAFS growth months are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly UNRATE decline below 3.5% signals tight labor markets that increase wage pressures and inflation, prompting central bank hawkishness and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in UNRATE < 3.5% periods exceed 2.0% (z-score > 2.0).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in industrial production (INDPRO) and rise in CPIAUCSL indicates stagflationary pressures, reducing corporate margins and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in stagflation scenarios exceed 1.0% (z-score > 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, declining GDP growth (GDPC1) signals slowing demand, which reduces earnings expectations and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in GDPC1 decline periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
